category.label: Algebraic Expressions
category.description: Structure, evaluation, simplification, and translation of algebraic expressions — covering notation, substitution, like-term collection, distribution, equivalent forms, verbal translation, operation order, absolute value expressions, and rational expression structure. These skills are the symbolic manipulation engine used in every downstream unit.
category.yield_rationale: F2 feeds distribution (Unit 1 polynomial work), equivalent expressions (1.11, 2.12, 3.12), substitution (2.7 composition, function evaluation), and verbal translation (FRQ modeling); medium-high load across all three AP Precalc units.

umbrellas.label: Expression Components
umbrellas.description: Recognizing the fundamental building blocks of algebraic expressions — variables, constants, coefficients (including implied), terms, factors, and monomials — without solving equations or analyzing functions.
umbrellas.yield_rationale: Background structural literacy; rarely the direct test object once functions are introduced, but prerequisite for all other umbrellas.

in_depth.label: Variables as quantities
in_depth.description: A variable represents an unknown, changing, or unspecified quantity inside an expression. Tests interpreting the role of a symbol before any manipulation.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Pure vocabulary — seldom tested directly at the AP level but anchors all symbolic fluency.

in_depth.label: Constants in expressions
in_depth.description: A constant is a fixed numerical value in an expression. Tests distinguishing standalone numbers from variable terms.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Foundational identification skill; low direct yield but used whenever students parse expression structure.

in_depth.label: Coefficients of variable terms
in_depth.description: A coefficient is the numerical factor multiplying a variable term, including positive, negative, fractional, decimal, and implied values. Tests correct coefficient identification in varied forms.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Coefficient reading is load-bearing in polynomial structure (Unit 1) and exponential parameter identification.

in_depth.label: Implied coefficient of 1
in_depth.description: A variable written without a visible coefficient has coefficient 1. Tests expressions like x, y, and ab where the 1 is invisible.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Prevents silent errors in term collection and distribution; moderate downstream usage.

in_depth.label: Implied coefficient of -1
in_depth.description: A variable term with only a leading negative sign has coefficient -1. Tests expressions like -x and -ab.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Sign-handling errors with implied -1 propagate into distribution and trig identity rewriting.

in_depth.label: Terms in algebraic expressions
in_depth.description: Terms are the additive parts of an expression separated by + or − at the top level (outside any grouping). Tests correct parsing of expression structure into its constituent terms.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Term-parsing is prerequisite for like-term collection and polynomial classification.

in_depth.label: Term signs in expressions
in_depth.description: Each term carries the sign directly preceding it, so 4x − 7y has terms +4x and −7y. Tests distinguishing signed terms to prevent collection errors.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Sign errors on terms are among the most common algebraic mistakes; directly exercised in polynomial addition and trig expression simplification.

in_depth.label: Factors in algebraic terms
in_depth.description: Factors are quantities multiplied together inside a single term. Tests recognizing the multiplicative structure within one term, such as 3, m, and n inside 3mn.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Factor recognition supports GCF extraction (polynomial factoring) and rational expression simplification.

umbrellas.label: Expression Notation and Grouping
umbrellas.description: Recognizing operation symbols, grouping symbols, implied multiplication, fraction bars, and boundary concepts (expression vs. equation, expression vs. term) — the notation layer of algebraic literacy.
umbrellas.yield_rationale: Notation literacy is prerequisite for distribution, nested expressions, and rational function notation; split from expression_components to keep each umbrella under 8 in_depth.

in_depth.label: Single-variable terms
in_depth.description: A single-variable term contains exactly one variable type, possibly with a coefficient or power. Tests distinguishing 5x from multi-variable terms like 5xy.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Definitional; low direct yield but supports like-term classification.

in_depth.label: Multi-variable terms
in_depth.description: A multi-variable term contains more than one variable factor, such as xy, 3ab, or -5mn. Tests identifying and classifying these compound terms.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Structural classification; moderate relevance to multi-variable polynomial and rational expressions.

in_depth.label: Monomial term structure
in_depth.description: A monomial is a single algebraic term made from a coefficient and variable factors. Tests structural recognition — distinguishing one term from multi-term expressions.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Monomial identification is the entry point for polynomial degree and classification (Unit 1).

in_depth.label: Expression versus equation
in_depth.description: An expression has no equals sign; an equation asserts two expressions are equal. Tests the structural distinction that separates simplification tasks from solving tasks.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Conceptual boundary prevents task-confusion errors throughout; appears in communication and reasoning (MP3).

in_depth.label: Expression versus term
in_depth.description: A term is one additive part of an expression; an expression may contain one or many terms. Tests local vs. whole-expression distinctions.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Mostly definitional; low direct AP yield.

in_depth.label: Operation symbols in expressions
in_depth.description: Algebraic expressions use symbols for addition, subtraction, multiplication, division, powers, and grouping. Tests reading which operation connects the parts of an expression.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Operation reading is background fluency needed for notation-heavy AP content.

in_depth.label: Implied multiplication notation
in_depth.description: Multiplication can appear without a symbol: 5x, 3(a + b), and ab all mean multiplication. Tests reading compact algebraic notation correctly.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Misreading implied multiplication causes systematic errors in distribution and function composition.

in_depth.label: Grouping symbols in algebraic expressions
in_depth.description: Parentheses, brackets, and braces group parts of an expression so they act as a single unit. Tests structural identification of grouped quantities before any operation is applied.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Grouping symbol literacy is prerequisite for distribution, nested expressions, and rational function notation.

in_depth.label: Fraction bar grouping with variables
in_depth.description: A fraction bar acts as a grouping symbol for the entire numerator and entire denominator. Tests parsing — distinguishing (x + 3)/5 from x + 3/5.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Fraction-bar parsing errors are a direct source of mistakes in rational expressions and logarithm arguments.

umbrellas.label: Substitution into Expressions
umbrellas.description: Replacing variables with given numeric values and evaluating the resulting algebraic structure — without solving for unknowns. Covers single-variable, multi-variable, negative, zero, fraction, decimal, and parenthesis-aware substitution.
umbrellas.yield_rationale: Function evaluation, composition (AP Precalc 2.7), and every FRQ model-application task use substitution; high load across the course.

in_depth.label: Single-variable numeric substitution
in_depth.description: A single numeric value replaces one variable throughout an expression. Tests the basic mechanics of expression evaluation at a given value.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Base-level function evaluation; exercised in virtually every AP Precalc topic.

in_depth.label: Multiple occurrences of the same variable
in_depth.description: When a variable appears more than once, the same replacement value must be substituted at every occurrence. Tests consistency across repeated variables.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Consistency errors compound in polynomial evaluation and multi-step function problems.

in_depth.label: Multi-variable numeric substitution
in_depth.description: Different variables in the same expression are each replaced by their corresponding given values. Tests matching each value to the correct variable.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Needed for multi-parameter function evaluation and model application (sinusoidal, exponential).

in_depth.label: Substitution of negative values
in_depth.description: Negative replacement values require careful parenthesization to preserve signs when multiplied or raised to powers. Tests sign accuracy during substitution.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Negative input errors are among the most frequent mistakes in function evaluation; persists throughout Units 1–3.

in_depth.label: Substitution of zero
in_depth.description: Substituting zero eliminates variable terms via multiplication but leaves constants unchanged. Tests awareness of which parts of an expression survive a zero substitution.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Zero substitution checks y-intercepts and zeros throughout polynomial and exponential function work.

in_depth.label: Substitution of fraction values
in_depth.description: Fractional values replace variables, requiring fraction multiplication and careful operation order. Tests substitution skill independent of standalone fraction arithmetic.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Fraction inputs appear in rational parameter problems and inverse function evaluation.

in_depth.label: Substitution of decimal values
in_depth.description: Decimal values replace variables, requiring place-value arithmetic within an algebraic expression. Tests decimal substitution as distinct from decimal arithmetic alone.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Decimal inputs appear in calculator-active sections and regression-model evaluation tasks.

in_depth.label: Parentheses during substitution
in_depth.description: Replacement values must be inserted with parentheses when signs, products, or powers could otherwise be misread. Tests correct grouping at the substitution step.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Parenthesis omission is the root cause of the most common evaluation sign errors in function problems.

umbrellas.label: Substitution: Order of Operations and Expression Forms
umbrellas.description: Applying substitution in structurally complex cases — operation order after replacement, power terms, grouped expressions, expression-for-variable, and formula evaluation. Follows on from basic numeric substitution.
umbrellas.yield_rationale: Advanced substitution forms are the direct bridge to function composition and formula evaluation; high load in AP Precalc Units 1–3. Split from substitution_into_expressions to keep each umbrella under 8 in_depth.

in_depth.label: Order of operations after substitution
in_depth.description: After replacing variables, the resulting numeric expression must be evaluated in correct PEMDAS order. Tests the connection between substitution and expression evaluation.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Operation-order errors after substitution affect every numeric answer in function evaluation contexts.

in_depth.label: Substitution into power terms
in_depth.description: When substituting into a term with an exponent, the replacement value becomes the base if grouped correctly. Tests the distinction between -x² and (-x)² at the substitution stage.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Power-term substitution errors are extremely common and persist into polynomial and exponential function evaluation.

in_depth.label: Substitution into grouped expressions
in_depth.description: Substituting inside parenthesized expressions requires preserving the grouped quantity before applying outside operations. Tests substitution into forms like 3(x + 4).
in_depth.yield_rationale: Grouped substitution is the mechanism behind composition evaluation and function-model application.

in_depth.label: Substituting an expression for a variable
in_depth.description: An entire algebraic expression replaces a variable rather than a single number. Tests expression replacement without conflating it with full function composition.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Direct precursor to function composition (AP Precalc 2.7, FRQ 1); high load in the course.

in_depth.label: Formula substitution
in_depth.description: Given values are inserted into a formula containing several variables. Tests using a formula as an algebraic expression to compute an output, not rearranging it.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Formula evaluation appears in modeling contexts (sinusoidal, exponential) and FRQ application parts.

umbrellas.label: Like Terms: Identification and Classification
umbrellas.description: Recognizing which terms are like and which are unlike — matching variable parts, powers, and variable combinations — as the prerequisite step before any combination.
umbrellas.yield_rationale: Needed for polynomial arithmetic (Unit 1) and simplifying log/trig expressions; medium load across the course.

in_depth.label: Like terms with the same variable part
in_depth.description: Like terms have exactly the same variable factors and powers; only their coefficients differ. Tests identifying like vs. unlike terms before any combination.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Correct like-term identification is prerequisite for every simplification task in the course.

in_depth.label: Unlike terms with different variables
in_depth.description: Terms with different variables cannot be combined. Tests that 4x and 4y remain separate and never merge into 8xy.
in_depth.yield_rationale: A persistent error that surfaces in multi-variable polynomial simplification.

in_depth.label: Unlike terms with different powers
in_depth.description: Terms sharing the same variable but different exponents are unlike and cannot be combined. Tests that x and x² remain separate.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Degree-confusion errors in polynomial simplification directly map to this skill.

in_depth.label: Unlike terms with different variable combinations
in_depth.description: Terms with partially overlapping variable sets (e.g., xy vs. x, or ab vs. ac) are unlike even when they share some variables. Tests finer-grained like-term classification.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Relevant to multi-variable polynomial and rational expression simplification.

in_depth.label: Integer coefficient combination
in_depth.description: Like terms with integer coefficients are combined by adding their coefficients while keeping the variable part unchanged. Tests the basic collect-like-terms algorithm.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Core simplification operation exercised throughout polynomial and trig expression work.

in_depth.label: Negative coefficient combination
in_depth.description: Like terms with negative coefficients require signed-number addition when combining. Tests sign accuracy in the collect-like-terms step.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Sign errors in collection are among the most common algebraic mistakes across all AP units.

in_depth.label: Fraction coefficient combination
in_depth.description: Like terms with fraction coefficients are combined by adding the fractional coefficients. Tests that the variable part is preserved while the fraction arithmetic is performed.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Fraction-coefficient collection appears in rational-parameter function simplification.

in_depth.label: Decimal coefficient combination
in_depth.description: Like terms with decimal coefficients are combined by decimal addition of the coefficients. Tests expression-level decimal arithmetic.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Decimal coefficients appear in regression-model simplification and data contexts.

umbrellas.label: Term Collection Strategies
umbrellas.description: Applying collection techniques — constant collection, multi-variable collection, reordering, additive-inverse cancellation, and post-grouping collection. Follows on from like-term identification.
umbrellas.yield_rationale: Collection techniques are exercised in every polynomial and trig simplification chain; split from like_terms_and_term_collection to keep each umbrella under 8 in_depth.

in_depth.label: Constant term collection
in_depth.description: Constant terms (no variable part) are like terms and can be combined with each other but not with variable terms. Tests combining pure-number terms in a mixed expression.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Constant collection completes every polynomial simplification and is used in sinusoidal parameter extraction.

in_depth.label: Multi-variable like term collection
in_depth.description: Terms with identical multi-variable parts (e.g., xy, ab) can be combined by their coefficients. Tests collection beyond single-variable contexts.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Multi-variable collection appears in polynomial products and trig identity simplification.

in_depth.label: Preserving unlike terms
in_depth.description: Unlike terms must remain separate in a simplified expression; combining them is an error. Tests recognizing which terms cannot be merged.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Prevents the persistent 'combine everything' error that corrupts rational and trig expression simplification.

in_depth.label: Term reordering for collection
in_depth.description: Terms can be rearranged using commutativity so like terms are adjacent and easier to collect. Tests that each term's sign travels with it when reordered.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Reordering strategy reduces errors in multi-term polynomial and logarithm simplification.

in_depth.label: Additive inverse like terms
in_depth.description: Like terms with equal but opposite coefficients (e.g., 5x and −5x) cancel completely to zero. Tests recognition of total cancellation.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Cancellation of opposite terms is the mechanism behind hole creation in rational functions and log identity simplification.

in_depth.label: Like terms across removed grouping
in_depth.description: After grouping symbols are correctly removed, like terms from inside and outside the former group may be collected. Tests combined structural rewriting and term collection.
in_depth.yield_rationale: This two-step skill (remove grouping, then collect) is exercised in every polynomial and trig simplification chain.

umbrellas.label: Distribution: Factor Types and Basic Forms
umbrellas.description: Using the distributive property with integer, negative, fraction, decimal, and variable factors — covering the basic monomial-over-binomial/trinomial, subtraction-of-group, and -1 distribution forms.
umbrellas.yield_rationale: Distribution is used in every AP Precalc unit to expand and simplify; it is a prerequisite for trig identity work (FRQ 4) and polynomial manipulation (Unit 1).

in_depth.label: Distribution by a positive integer
in_depth.description: A positive integer outside parentheses multiplies each term inside. Tests the basic distributive structure with the simplest factor type.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Foundation case of distribution; exercised in every polynomial expansion.

in_depth.label: Distribution by a negative integer
in_depth.description: A negative integer outside parentheses multiplies each inside term, flipping the sign of every term. Tests sign-change accuracy under negative distribution.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Negative distribution errors are among the most frequent algebraic mistakes; propagate directly into polynomial and trig expression work.

in_depth.label: Distribution by a fraction coefficient
in_depth.description: A fractional coefficient outside parentheses multiplies every term inside. Tests distribution when the factor is not a whole number.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Fraction distribution appears in rational-parameter equation solving and clearing fractions from expressions.

in_depth.label: Distribution by a decimal coefficient
in_depth.description: A decimal coefficient outside parentheses multiplies each term inside. Tests distribution combined with decimal arithmetic.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Decimal distribution appears in data-based modeling contexts and regression work.

in_depth.label: Distribution by a variable factor
in_depth.description: A variable factor outside parentheses multiplies each inside term — basic monomial distribution not involving polynomial-degree operations.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Variable-factor distribution is the first step in polynomial multiplication and rational expression expansion.

in_depth.label: Monomial distribution over a binomial
in_depth.description: A single term (numeric or variable) outside parentheses multiplies both terms of a two-term group. Tests the standard monomial × binomial expansion.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Monomial-binomial distribution is the core operation in polynomial arithmetic (Unit 1) and log manipulation (2.12).

in_depth.label: Monomial distribution over a trinomial
in_depth.description: A single term outside parentheses multiplies each of three terms inside. Tests complete distribution without polynomial factoring.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Three-term distribution is needed in polynomial GCF factoring and trig expression rewriting.

in_depth.label: Distribution over subtraction
in_depth.description: Distribution over a subtracted group requires multiplying the subtracted term with its sign included. Tests sign preservation inside minus-signed groups.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Subtraction-group sign errors are a root cause of mistakes in factored polynomial expansion and identity verification.

umbrellas.label: Distribution: Negation, Multi-Group, Nested, and Error Recognition
umbrellas.description: Advanced distribution patterns — negating grouped expressions, distributing across multiple groups, nested layers, chaining distribution with collection, and identifying partial/sign-error mistakes.
umbrellas.yield_rationale: Advanced distribution forms underlie polynomial subtraction, trig identity verification, and the AP MC error-recognition item type; split from distribution_and_expansion to keep each umbrella under 8 in_depth.

in_depth.label: Distribution by -1
in_depth.description: A lone negative sign outside parentheses acts as multiplication by -1, flipping every term's sign. Tests the negation-of-group rule.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Negating a grouped expression is one of the most error-prone moves in trig identity proofs and polynomial subtraction.

in_depth.label: Subtraction of a parenthesized expression
in_depth.description: Subtracting a parenthesized expression changes the sign of every term inside the group. Tests the two-step parse-then-negate rewrite.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Polynomial subtraction and completing the square both require this skill; high frequency in Units 1–2.

in_depth.label: Multiple separate distribution terms
in_depth.description: An expression containing more than one grouped part requires separate distributions before collecting terms. Tests multi-step expansion.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Multi-group distribution is the standard form of binomial products and transformed function expansions.

in_depth.label: Nested distribution
in_depth.description: Nested grouping symbols require distributing through one layer at a time, innermost first. Tests layered expansion without solving.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Nested distribution appears in complex rational expression expansion and multi-step identity verification.

in_depth.label: Distribution followed by term collection
in_depth.description: After distributing, like terms may need to be collected to produce the simplified equivalent form. Tests chaining expansion with simplification.
in_depth.yield_rationale: The expand-and-collect two-step is the workhorse of polynomial simplification throughout Unit 1 and FRQ 4.

in_depth.label: Partial distribution error recognition
in_depth.description: Distribution must reach every term inside the group; applying it only to the first term is an error. Tests recognition and correction of incomplete expansion.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Partial-distribution errors corrupt polynomial and trig expansions; error-recognition problems appear in AP MC.

in_depth.label: Distribution sign error recognition
in_depth.description: Negative distribution and group subtraction require sign changes on every affected term; missing sign changes are a specific error class. Tests identifying incorrect signs after expansion.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Sign errors after negative distribution are one of the most tested 'identify the mistake' scenarios in AP MC.

umbrellas.label: Equivalent Expressions: Definition, Verification, and Matching
umbrellas.description: Recognizing when two algebraic expressions have the same value for all allowed inputs — covering the equivalence concept, collection and distribution as rewrites, verification by substitution, counterexamples, and coefficient matching.
umbrellas.yield_rationale: Underpins AP Precalc 1.11, 2.12, and 3.12 — all 'equivalent representation' topics; high load throughout the course.

in_depth.label: Equivalent expressions
in_depth.description: Two expressions are equivalent when they produce the same value for every allowed input. Tests expression comparison as distinct from equation solving.
in_depth.yield_rationale: The conceptual anchor for all of 1.11, 2.12, and 3.12 (equivalent representations topics).

in_depth.label: Equivalence through like-term collection
in_depth.description: One expression is equivalent to another when it is produced by collecting like terms. Tests recognizing collection as a valid structural rewrite.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Collection-based equivalence is exercised in polynomial and log expression simplification.

in_depth.label: Equivalence through distribution
in_depth.description: A distributed (or factored) form of an expression is equivalent to its grouped form. Tests recognizing distribution and factoring as inverse structural rewrites.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Distribution equivalence is the mechanism behind 1.11 (polynomial/rational equivalent forms) and FRQ 4 symbolic manipulations.

in_depth.label: Testing equivalence by substitution
in_depth.description: Substituting the same value into two expressions checks whether they agree at that input — a necessary but not sufficient test of full equivalence. Tests the substitution-as-verification strategy.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Substitution-check reasoning appears in AP MC equivalence questions and MP3 justification tasks.

in_depth.label: Non-equivalence from a counterexample
in_depth.description: A single input value producing different outputs in two expressions proves non-equivalence. Tests the counterexample approach to disproving equivalence.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Counterexample reasoning is explicitly tested in AP Precalc communication and reasoning items (MP3).

in_depth.label: Coefficient matching in equivalent linear expressions
in_depth.description: Two equivalent linear expressions must have identical variable coefficients and identical constant terms. Tests structural comparison without solving an equation.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Coefficient matching is used in parameter identification for function models across Units 1–3.

umbrellas.label: Algebraic Arithmetic Properties
umbrellas.description: Commutative, associative, identity, and zero-factor properties applied to algebraic expressions — the structural rules that justify rearranging and regrouping without changing value.
umbrellas.yield_rationale: Arithmetic properties underlie strategic simplification moves throughout the course; split from equivalent_expression_properties to keep each umbrella under 8 in_depth.

in_depth.label: Commutative property of algebraic addition
in_depth.description: Additive terms can be reordered without changing the expression's value, provided each term keeps its sign. Tests applying commutativity to algebraic sums.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Commutativity enables term reordering for easier collection; background skill in simplification.

in_depth.label: Commutative property of algebraic multiplication
in_depth.description: Factors in a product can be reordered without changing the product's value, supporting rewrites like 3x = x·3 and ab = ba. Tests commutative rewriting of algebraic products.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Supports factor reordering in GCF extraction and monomial simplification.

in_depth.label: Associative property of algebraic addition
in_depth.description: Additive grouping can change without changing the sum: (x + 3) + 5 = x + (3 + 5). Tests associative regrouping in algebraic sums.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Enables strategic regrouping for efficient simplification; background skill.

in_depth.label: Associative property of algebraic multiplication
in_depth.description: Multiplicative grouping can change without changing the product: (3x)y = 3(xy). Tests associative regrouping of algebraic factors.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Supports coefficient extraction and factor regrouping in polynomial and rational expressions.

in_depth.label: Identity properties in algebraic expressions
in_depth.description: Adding 0 or multiplying by 1 leaves any algebraic expression unchanged. Tests recognizing these identity-based equivalences.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Identity properties are used in completing the square and adding/subtracting zero strategically in proofs.

in_depth.label: Zero factor in algebraic expressions
in_depth.description: Any algebraic expression multiplied by 0 equals 0. Tests expression-structure recognition — not the zero-product property for equations.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Background property; supports understanding cancellation and coefficient interpretation.

umbrellas.label: Verbal-to-Symbolic: Basic Operations and Order
umbrellas.description: Translating addition, subtraction, multiplication, division, and power phrases into algebraic expressions — with particular attention to order errors in subtraction and division phrases.
umbrellas.yield_rationale: FRQ modeling tasks (1.14, 2.5, 2.14, 3.7) require translating contextual language into function notation; moderate-high load for the modeling FRQs.

in_depth.label: Addition phrase expressions
in_depth.description: Phrases describing sums, totals, increases, or quantities added together translate to algebraic addition. Tests symbolic expression construction from additive language.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Additive translation is background for function model construction in FRQ 2 and 3.

in_depth.label: Subtraction phrase expressions
in_depth.description: Phrases describing differences, decreases, or quantities less than another translate to algebraic subtraction. Tests correct operand order in subtraction expressions.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Subtraction order errors are a frequent source of reversed expressions in modeling problems.

in_depth.label: Less-than phrase order
in_depth.description: Phrases like '5 less than x' reverse the spoken order, producing x − 5 not 5 − x. Tests a specific high-frequency translation misconception.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Less-than order reversal is the single most common verbal-to-symbolic translation error in precalc and AP contexts.

in_depth.label: More-than phrase order
in_depth.description: Phrases like '6 more than x' produce x + 6; because addition is commutative, order is less critical but the relationship must be interpreted correctly.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Complementary to less-than phrase order; tests correct additive relationship parsing.

in_depth.label: Multiplication phrase expressions
in_depth.description: Phrases describing products, multiples, or scaled quantities translate to algebraic multiplication. Includes forms like 3x, ab, and 5(n + 1).
in_depth.yield_rationale: Multiplicative translation feeds coefficient-of-a-function-rule and scaled-model construction.

in_depth.label: Division phrase expressions
in_depth.description: Phrases describing quotients or ratios translate to algebraic division. Tests correct placement of dividend and divisor in the expression.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Division translation is needed for rational expression construction and per-unit rate modeling.

in_depth.label: Quotient phrase order
in_depth.description: Quotient phrases preserve which quantity is divided by which; order errors invert the expression. Tests the non-commutative nature of division in verbal descriptions.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Inverted quotients produce wrong rational expressions; this error appears in FRQ modeling and MC interpretation.

in_depth.label: Power phrase expressions
in_depth.description: Words like 'square', 'cube', or 'raised to a power' translate to exponent notation. Tests translation to exponential form without applying exponent laws.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Power translation is used in contextual polynomial and exponential model construction.

umbrellas.label: Verbal-to-Symbolic: Grouping, Sequences, and Contextual Expressions
umbrellas.description: Translating phrases that require grouping (sums and differences treated as single units), consecutive-integer patterns, percent language, and unit-rate contexts into algebraic expressions.
umbrellas.yield_rationale: Grouped translation maps directly to transformation parameters in function models; percent and rate language appears in exponential and linear FRQs. Split from verbal_to_symbolic_expression_structure to keep each umbrella under 8 in_depth.

in_depth.label: Grouped sum phrase expressions
in_depth.description: Phrases treating a sum as a single quantity (e.g., 'three times the sum of x and 4') require parentheses in the algebraic form. Tests grouping-aware translation.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Grouping in translation directly maps to transformation parameters (A, B, C, D) in function model construction.

in_depth.label: Grouped difference phrase expressions
in_depth.description: Phrases treating a difference as one quantity require parentheses when the difference is multiplied or divided. Tests sign-preserving grouped translation.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Grouped-difference translation errors cause sign mistakes in model parameter extraction.

in_depth.label: Consecutive integer expressions
in_depth.description: Consecutive integers are represented using a starting variable with increments of 1 (e.g., n, n+1, n+2). Tests constructing multi-term expressions for sequences.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Appears in sequence-to-expression bridges; moderate precalc load, lower direct AP yield.

in_depth.label: Consecutive even integer expressions
in_depth.description: Consecutive even integers differ by 2 and are expressed as n, n+2, n+4 when n is even. Tests constructing even-step increment expressions.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Useful for arithmetic sequence bridges but low direct AP Precalc exam yield.

in_depth.label: Consecutive odd integer expressions
in_depth.description: Consecutive odd integers differ by 2 and are expressed as n, n+2, n+4 when n is odd. Tests constructing odd-step increment expressions.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Parallel to even-integer structure; low direct AP yield.

in_depth.label: Percent of a variable quantity
in_depth.description: A percent of an unknown is written by converting the percent to a decimal or fraction multiplier. Tests expression construction for percentage relationships.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Growth/decay percent language in exponential models (2.3, 2.5) uses this translation pattern.

in_depth.label: Variable expression from a unit rate
in_depth.description: A unit rate multiplied by a variable quantity produces an algebraic expression for a total. Tests expression construction in linear rate contexts.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Unit-rate expressions are the building blocks of linear function models and appear in FRQ 2 setup.

umbrellas.label: Algebraic Expression Order and Grouping
umbrellas.description: Interpreting operation order, grouping symbols, negative signs, and power scopes in algebraic expressions. Tests which part of an expression is acted on first — prerequisite for correct parsing of rational functions and logarithm arguments.
umbrellas.yield_rationale: Order-and-grouping literacy underlies rational function work (1.7–1.10) and logarithm manipulation (2.12); referenced in F2 rationale for 1.11 and 3.12.

in_depth.label: Operation precedence with variables
in_depth.description: Algebraic expressions follow the same PEMDAS order as numeric expressions. Tests identifying which operation applies first when variables are present.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Precedence with variables is load-bearing for correct evaluation in every AP unit.

in_depth.label: Grouping precedence with variables
in_depth.description: A grouped algebraic quantity is treated as a unit before any outside operation is applied. Tests parsing the interior of a parenthesized group before acting on it.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Grouping precedence errors produce systematic distribution and substitution mistakes throughout the course.

in_depth.label: Nested grouping with variables
in_depth.description: Expressions with grouping symbols inside other grouping symbols require interpreting the innermost group first. Tests layered precedence in algebraic notation.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Nested grouping appears in complex rational expressions and trig expression simplification.

in_depth.label: Coefficient attached to a group
in_depth.description: A coefficient directly adjacent to parentheses multiplies the entire grouped expression. Tests correct parsing before distribution is applied.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Misidentifying the scope of a coefficient causes distribution errors; directly relevant to polynomial and trig expansion.

in_depth.label: Power applied to variable versus coefficient
in_depth.description: In 3x², the exponent applies only to x unless parentheses show otherwise; (3x)² applies to the whole term. Tests the scope of an exponent in a monomial.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Exponent-scope misreading is a top-three source of errors in polynomial evaluation and power rule application.

in_depth.label: Negative variable power grouping
in_depth.description: Parentheses determine whether a negative sign is inside or outside a power: -x² vs. (-x)². Tests the structural distinction at the base level of a power.
in_depth.yield_rationale: This distinction causes one of the most common evaluation errors in polynomial and exponential problems on the AP exam.

in_depth.label: Fraction coefficient versus division structure
in_depth.description: A fraction adjacent to a variable may be a coefficient (½x) or the expression may divide an entire group ((x+2)/5). Tests distinguishing these two fraction-in-expression structures.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Fraction-structure misreading produces wrong rational expressions; directly relevant to 1.7–1.10 and logarithm arguments.

in_depth.label: Ambiguous linear expression grouping
in_depth.description: Written forms like x + 3/2 and (x + 3)/2 have different structures; the grouping must be explicit to resolve the ambiguity. Tests precision in reading and writing algebraic notation.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Notation precision is tested in AP Precalc communication tasks and is prerequisite for unambiguous function formula writing.

umbrellas.label: Absolute Value Expressions
umbrellas.description: Interpreting, evaluating, and writing algebraic absolute value expressions — applying the absolute value rule to variable expressions, composing absolute value with linear forms, and recognizing key structural properties. This is expression-level work, not absolute value equation/inequality solving.
umbrellas.yield_rationale: Absolute value expressions appear in piecewise function definitions (F7), distance-related modeling, and as notation within AP Precalc arguments; moderate foundation load.

in_depth.label: Absolute value expression definition
in_depth.description: The absolute value |a| of a real number returns the non-negative distance from zero. Tests applying the definition to numeric and simple variable expressions.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Definitional; used whenever absolute value appears in function notation or expressions.

in_depth.label: Absolute value of a variable expression
in_depth.description: An absolute value sign applied to an algebraic expression such as |3x − 2| produces a non-negative output for every input. Tests evaluating |f(x)| at given values without solving equations.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Evaluating absolute value expressions is prerequisite for piecewise definitions and distance modeling contexts.

in_depth.label: Absolute value of a negative expression
in_depth.description: When the expression inside absolute value bars is negative, the output equals its opposite. Tests applying the two-case output rule to expressions with negative values.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Negative-input absolute value behavior is tested whenever students evaluate piecewise or transformed absolute value forms.

in_depth.label: Absolute value bars as grouping
in_depth.description: Absolute value bars group their entire contents as a unit before the absolute value is applied, similar to parentheses. Tests correct parsing when the argument is a multi-term expression.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Grouping errors inside absolute value bars cause evaluation mistakes; relevant to piecewise and inequality notation.

in_depth.label: Absolute value symmetry property
in_depth.description: |−x| = |x| for all real x; absolute value of an expression and its negation are equal. Tests the structural sign-independence property.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Symmetry property is used in simplifying absolute value expressions and in trig-context distance reasoning.

umbrellas.label: Rational Expression Structure
umbrellas.description: Recognizing, reading, and writing rational expressions — algebraic fractions with polynomial numerators and denominators. Covers identifying the domain restriction (denominator ≠ 0), simplifying by canceling common factors, and distinguishing rational from non-rational forms. This is expression structure, not full rational-function analysis.
umbrellas.yield_rationale: Rational expression fluency is direct prerequisite for 1.7–1.11 (rational functions unit) and for simplifying log and trig expressions with fraction forms; high load in Unit 1.

in_depth.label: Rational expression definition
in_depth.description: A rational expression is a ratio of two polynomial expressions. Tests identifying which algebraic fractions qualify as rational and which do not (e.g., those with radicals or variables in exponents).
in_depth.yield_rationale: Definitional gating for Unit 1 rational function work; students must distinguish rational from non-rational forms.

in_depth.label: Domain restriction from denominator
in_depth.description: A rational expression is undefined when its denominator equals zero. Tests identifying the excluded values from the domain of a rational expression.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Domain restriction from denominator = 0 is the direct precursor to vertical asymptote identification (AP Precalc 1.9).

in_depth.label: Numerator and denominator as polynomial expressions
in_depth.description: The numerator and denominator of a rational expression are each polynomial expressions that must be treated as grouped units. Tests structural parsing of the two-part fraction form.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Structural parsing is prerequisite for degree comparison (end behavior, 1.7) and for partial fraction setup (1.11).

in_depth.label: Equivalent forms of a rational expression
in_depth.description: A rational expression can be rewritten by multiplying numerator and denominator by the same nonzero factor, producing an equivalent form. Tests the algebraic-fraction equivalence rule.
in_depth.yield_rationale: Equivalent forms are used in partial fraction decomposition and in combining rational expressions with different denominators.
